subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the story of Rakesh and the cherry tree
Learn the importance of patience and care in nurturing plants
Identify the main characters and events in the story
Understand how nature grows despite challenges
Recognize the relationship between humans and nature
Develop reading comprehension and vocabulary skills

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Plants and Nature
teaching_strategy: Class discussion about plants and trees
Teacher asking introductory questions from the textbook
Short explanation about the author Ruskin Bond
student_activities: Share experiences of planting seeds or saplings
Name fruit trees found in their surroundings

lesson_plan.title: Beginning of the Story
teaching_strategy: Guided reading of the first part of the story
student_activities: Explain why Rakesh planted the seed

lesson_plan.title: Growth of the Cherry Tree
topics_covered: Care and watering of the tree
student_activities: List ways to care for plants
Draw a simple picture of the cherry plant

lesson_plan.title: Challenges Faced by the Tree
student_activities: Discuss how the tree survived difficulties
Explain why the cherry tree is strong

lesson_plan.title: Blossoms and Fruits
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of the story climax
Reading important passages from the text
student_activities: Describe the beauty of the cherry tree
Discuss why the tree became special to Rakesh

lesson_plan.title: Message and Language Activities
teaching_strategy: Teacher explaining the moral of the story
student_activities: Write the moral of the story
Complete grammar exercises from the textbook

teacher_tip: Encourage students to plant a small seed or sapling and observe its growth. This activity helps them understand the message of patience and care shown in the story.
